methods.description: Process for collecting dead coral rubble with A. lobifera attached from various reef sites and preparing them for further analysis.

steps.description: Dead coral rubble with A. lobifera attached was collected from inner-, mid-and outer-shelf reefs located on the northern GBR in August 2014.
steps.searchable_summary: collect coral rubble samples from specified reef sites

steps.description: Samples were collected by SCUBA divers from the back slope of reefs with similar habitat located on the (1) 3"E) at depths of 6 to 8 m (corrected to lowest astronomical tide levels).
steps.searchable_summary: collect samples at specified depths using SCUBA

steps.description: Pieces of dead coral rubble were brought to the laboratory located at the Lizard Island Research Station and were scrubbed to remove LBF.
steps.searchable_summary: transport and clean coral rubble samples

steps.description: The resultant sediment was transferred to glass Petri dishes and was placed undisturbed in a flowthrough aquarium system.
steps.searchable_summary: transfer sediment to Petri dishes and place in aquarium

steps.description: A total of five A. lobifera individuals per reef site were extracted and cleaned using a fine brush (#000) under a stereoscope to remove any particles from the test exterior and photographed.
steps.searchable_summary: extract and clean A. lobifera individuals, then photograph

steps.description: Specimens were then snap frozen in liquid N2 and kept at −80°C until analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: snap freeze specimens and store at -80°C
